examples.label: Target present — middle
examples.notes: 23 is at index 5. Midpoint probe hits it on the first or second iteration.

examples.label: Target present — first element
examples.notes: Target at the leftmost position; search converges from the left.

examples.label: Target present — last element
examples.notes: Target at the rightmost position; search converges from the right.

examples.label: Target absent
examples.notes: 25 is not in the array. Algorithm exhausts the search space and returns -1.

examples.label: Empty array
examples.notes: The loop body never executes; -1 is returned immediately.

examples.label: Single element — found
examples.notes: Trivial case: one probe finds the element.

examples.label: Duplicates
examples.notes: With duplicates, binary search may return ANY valid index of the target.

precondition: Array must be sorted in non-decreasing order.
postcondition: Returns index i with arr[i]==target, or -1 if absent.
invariant: If target exists, it lies within arr[low..high] at every iteration.
reference: Knuth, D.E. (1998). The Art of Computer Programming, Vol. 3, §6.2.1
